tasks.title: Reframe the Experience timeline around builder arcs
tasks.rationale: The hero now communicates Sharang's identity much more clearly, but the Experience section still reads mainly as chronology. Reframing it around recurring builder arcs can connect Luma AI, Persona, AISOC, OpenShiksha, VaccinePost, and the Games section into a more coherent story about ambitious systems, public-interest motivation, and creative product instincts.
acceptanceCriteria: The Experience section introduces two or three clear through-lines that connect current AI product work, public-interest or open-source work, and creative interaction design.
The timeline remains factually grounded in existing roles and projects such as Luma AI, Persona, AISOC, OpenShiksha, and VaccinePost without adding unsupported claims.
The revised structure makes the transition into the Games section feel intentional rather than disconnected.
Desktop and mobile layouts stay readable and responsive without breaking the current timeline alignment or creating horizontal overflow.
Dark mode, light mode, keyboard focus states, and contrast all remain accessible.
notes: Implemented a new builder-arc frame above the Experience timeline, rewrote the timeline as a structured narrative around recurring arcs, and added a deliberate bridge into the Games section.
Added AISOC to the Experience narrative, renamed the final timeline chapter to public-interest and open source, and tightened copy for Luma AI, Persona, OpenShiksha, and VaccinePost.
Updated the Games introduction copy so it reads as a design lab connected to the rest of Sharang's builder identity.

This run is blocked on environment constraints: ntfy posts failed with a transport receive error, the installed Node runtime in Program Files was inaccessible from the sandbox, and git push over SSH failed with a signal-pipe error.

tasks.title: Design a Builder Modes narrative section
tasks.rationale: The new control-room intro improved the first impression, but the middle of the page still collapses into a generic resume pattern. Reframing the Technical and In My Down Time area around how Sharang builds can better connect his Luma product work, public-interest motivation, open-source contributions, and game-making practice.
acceptanceCriteria: The section clearly presents three connected modes of work: AI product systems, public-interest or open-source building, and creative play.
Examples use grounded portfolio facts such as Luma AI, Persona, OpenShiksha, VaccinePost, AISOC, and existing game projects without adding unsupported claims.
Raw tool lists are either compressed or subordinated so the narrative leads and technical credibility still remains visible.
Desktop and mobile layouts remain responsive, readable, and visually consistent with the control-room introduction.
The Games section feels more connected to the rest of the portfolio rather than reading as an isolated extra.

tasks.title: Build a personal control-room intro system
tasks.rationale: The current portfolio is polished but reads too much like a generic product site. A stronger first-screen narrative can better unify Sharang's AI engineering depth, social-impact motivation, and creative interests without changing the site's one-page structure.
acceptanceCriteria: The first viewport clearly explains who Sharang is, what he is building now, and why his work matters.
The intro uses factual personal signals that connect AI/product work with games, sports, and board-game interests.
Desktop and mobile layouts remain responsive and visually intentional.
Dark mode, light mode, and keyboard focus states remain accessible and legible.
The resulting design avoids generic SaaS styling and avoids adding unsupported claims.
notes: Implemented a new control-room hero, refreshed the profile rail, tightened narrative copy, and reframed the Games section so it reads as part of Sharang's identity.
Added stronger focus states and keyboard handling for interactive elements, including game cards and timeline links.
Updated metadata to better match the portfolio's AI-engineer-plus-creative-builder positioning.
Fixed a Mixpanel initialization crash in App.js so analytics calls do not break portfolio interactions when the token is unavailable.
Opened PR #2 with screenshot artifacts linked in the body.
Follow-up review fixes reordered the mobile stack so the control-room hero leads on small screens and promoted the major resume section labels to semantic h2 headings.
